``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined The largest broad class of sentences offer parole release eligibility after one-quarter of the judicial maximum term has been served. Good time deductions typically result in mandatory release dates at 67-percent of the maximum.

## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 17%, changing from 5,597 in 2010 to 4,639 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series

``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity

``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 56%, dropping from 12,955 in 2010 to 5,715 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among Other race(s), non-Hispanic and white, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Alaska, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 5.2 times higher and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 3.3 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served ::: {.cell} ::: Hispanic, any race individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 1 years. Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 0.6 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Murder and Non-negligent Manslaughter offenses, Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals had 5.34 more years on average compared to White, non-Hispanic individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
